title: FLUX.2 Klein Character LoRA
description: Identity-focused LoRA recipe for learning a portable character without overbinding pose, clothing, background, or rendering style.

dataset.preferredImages: 20-80 images with varied pose, distance, expression, clothing, and background
notes: Vary pose, crop, scene, and medium if portability matters.
Avoid many same-background images unless the background is part of the character concept.

captioning.technique: Use a unique trigger token plus a concrete class noun, then repeat the stable identity anchors in every caption and describe the variables in that image.
rules: Start every caption with the trigger token.
Include a class noun such as man, woman, robot, creature, dog, or character.
Repeat stable identity traits in every caption.
Describe pose, clothing, expression, camera distance, background, and medium as variables.
Never write same person, same character, previous image, or another view; captions are independent examples.
Do not over-describe temporary props unless they should become identity traits.
examples: fieldperson, gray-bearded fisherman man wearing a yellow rain hat and blue rubber overalls, holding a fishing rod on a wooden dock
fieldperson, gray-bearded fisherman man wearing a yellow rain hat and blue rubber overalls, close-up portrait with weathered face against a plain gray background

prompts: CHAR_TRIGGER, gray-bearded fisherman man wearing a black wool coat in a snowy city street, documentary photograph
CHAR_TRIGGER, gray-bearded fisherman man as a small bronze statue on a museum pedestal, studio lighting
CHAR_TRIGGER, gray-bearded fisherman man wearing a yellow rain hat piloting a tiny spaceship, colorful comic panel
